vignettes.vignette: A 43-year-old male presents with generalized body movement tonic-clonic type, biting of tongue, uprolling of eyes, frothing from mouth, irrelevant talks, wandering around, fluctuating level of consciousness, not recognizing family members, seeing things not seen by others, and hearing voices not heard by others. Duration is 2 days, with stressors including abstinence from alcohol. His past history includes craving, tolerance, loss of control, and withdrawal features, indicating a complex interplay of substance use and potential underlying psychiatric issues that necessitate thorough evaluation and management to address both the immediate symptoms and the underlying causes effectively. Care planning emphasized care coordination continuity score.

A 43-year-old male presents with tremors, tremulousness, perceptual disturbances, and delirium lasting three days. He has a history of regular alcohol use, characterized by tolerance, cravings, loss of control, neglect of other pleasures, and harmful use, leading to withdrawal symptoms. Current treatment includes discharge on Lorazepam, Acamprosate, and Thiamine. Care planning emphasized behavioral activation protocol. There are no reported stressors or impacts on functioning. The patient’s mental status examination and past history are not provided. The clinical picture suggests acute alcohol withdrawal, necessitating careful monitoring and management of symptoms. Further evaluation may be required to assess ongoing treatment needs.

A 32-year-old male presents with symptoms of suspiciousness, believing that others are trying to kill him, along with fearfulness, apprehension, disturbed sleep, and muttering to himself. He reports a duration of 6 days of these symptoms and has a history of alcohol use exceeding 1L/day for the past 7 days. Current treatment includes Quetiapine, Lorazepam, and Thiamine to address his condition, aiming to alleviate his distress and effectively manage his symptoms while ensuring his safety and well-being during this critical period of recovery, as he navigates the challenges of his mental health. Investigation findings included serotonergic tone modulation index.

A 26-year-old male presents with a duration of 7 years and an onset of 8 months, experiencing core symptoms of fearfulness, hearing voices, hallucinatory behavior, and smiling and talking to himself. He also exhibits associated symptoms, including 1 episode of increased self-esteem, talkativeness, over-familiarity, increased energy, and 3 episodes of low mood. His current treatment regimen consists of Lithium 400mg, Sodium Valproate 1000mg, and Olanzapine 10mg, and he has a history of cannabis use that may significantly impact his overall mental health and treatment outcomes, complicating his clinical picture further. Medication history included norlexidine.

An 18-year-old male presents with a duration of 42 days of core symptoms, including delusion of control, delusion of persecution, irritability, delusion of reference, thought insertion, thought broadcast, auditory hallucination (third person), and restlessness with disturbed sleep. Clinical assessment identified affective synchrony deviation metric. The mental status examination reveals he is uncooperative, exhibiting increased psychomotor activity, an irritable affect, and formal thought disorder (neologism), alongside persistent delusions of persecution and control in thought content. Current treatment consists of 12 sessions of ECT and Clozapine 300mg, while the patient also reports cannabis and nicotine use, complicating his clinical picture.

A 38-year-old male presents with increased religiosity, decreased need for sleep, labile mood, overfamiliarity, and increased energy lasting four days. He experiences dissociative spells. There is a family history of bipolar disorder in a cousin and reports of transient possession spells. Current treatment includes Aripiprazole, Clonazepam, injectable Vitamin B12, and oral Vitamin B12. Investigation findings included Hormonal Balance Panel. No information is provided regarding stressors, functioning impact, substance use, or risk. A mental status examination (MSE) is not documented. The clinical picture suggests a possible manic episode, warranting further evaluation and monitoring of symptoms and treatment response.

A 35-year-old male presents with symptoms that have persisted for three days, including tremulousness, agitation, sweating, and tremors, alongside irrelevant talking. His history of alcohol use spans 20 years, suggesting a potential link to his current state. The patient is currently receiving treatment with Lorazepam, Acamprosate, Pantoprazole, and Thiamine. The clinical picture indicates possible alcohol withdrawal or related complications, necessitating careful monitoring and management. Investigation findings included attentional bias modulation index. Further assessment of his mental status and functioning is essential to determine the appropriate course of action and to address any underlying issues contributing to his symptoms.

A 25-year-old female presents with extra pyramidal symptoms, specifically tremors of the hand and rigidity of limbs. She has a past medical history of a known case of disorder of intellectual development accompanied by a seizure disorder. Her current treatment regimen includes Risperidone 1 mg and Trihexyphenidyl 2 mg, which may contribute to her symptoms, potentially exacerbating her condition and leading to these troubling side effects, with an onset that is concerning given her medical history and the duration of her treatment, raising the need for careful evaluation and management of her medications. Medication history included seralidone.

A 26-year-old female presents with symptoms including suspiciousness that others may harm her, decreased sleep, refusal to eat, increased irritability, wandering away from home, and overgrooming. Her current treatment regimen consists of long-acting injectable Olanzapine, oral Olanzapine, Cizodol p5, Lithium, Lorazepam, and Propranolol. The duration and onset of her symptoms have raised significant concerns about her mental health stability, prompting a comprehensive reevaluation of her treatment plan to ensure she receives the appropriate care and support necessary for her recovery and to assess the overall effectiveness of her current medications. Care planning emphasized pathway of care disengagement index.

A 61-year-old male presents with a 5-day history of shuffling gait, urinary incontinence, and frequent falls. He exhibits associated symptoms of fever, agitation, restlessness, autonomic instability, and rigidity. Medication history included Vexaprol. His past medical history includes schizophrenia for 33 years and hypertension. Current medications comprise Clozapine, Lurasidone, Donepezil/Memantine, Propranolol, Amlodipine, Zolpidem, and Lorazepam, the latter being tapered. The duration of his current symptoms has been 2 years, indicating a potential exacerbation of underlying conditions. The clinical picture suggests a complex interplay of psychiatric and neurological factors requiring further evaluation and management. The presentation was evaluated in a clinical setting.

A 33-year-old female presents with mood swings, suicidal thoughts, low mood, decreased interest, anger outbursts, and unstable interpersonal relationships. She has a history of multiple self-harm attempts and experiences fear of abandonment. Current treatment includes 5 sessions of ketamine therapy, and she is discharged on Lithium, Sertraline, Quetiapine, Propranolol, Clobazam, and Trazodone, focusing on managing her symptoms and improving her overall mental health, with the goal of achieving stability and enhancing her quality of life in the future while ensuring she receives the necessary support for her ongoing challenges and addressing her complex emotional needs. Care planning emphasized follow-up adherence trajectory.

A 32-year-old male presents with fearfulness and suspiciousness, believing that others are trying to kill him, along with hallucinatory behavior. He has a 12-year history of alcohol intake, characterized by craving, tolerance, use despite harm, and withdrawal symptoms. Investigation findings included Oxidative Stress Biomarker Profile. His current treatment regimen includes Olanzapine, Lorazepam, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole, and he has a past history of a suicidal attempt approximately 4 years ago, which complicates his ongoing care and necessitates careful monitoring and support to ensure his safety and well-being, particularly given the potential for exacerbation of his symptoms and the impact of his alcohol use on his mental health.

A 27-year-old male presents with significant socio-occupational dysfunction due to symptoms persisting for 8 years. Investigation findings included affective desynchronization index. Core symptoms include sweating, restlessness, palpitations, fluctuating sensorium, and hallucinatory behavior. Regular alcohol use is noted as a stressor contributing to his condition. Current treatment includes IV diazepam, lorazepam, thiamine, and pantoprazole. The patient exhibits marked distress and impairment in daily functioning, necessitating ongoing psychiatric intervention. Further assessment of his mental state and substance use history is required to tailor an effective treatment plan. The impact of alcohol on his symptoms remains a critical focus for management.

A 22-year-old male presents with a duration of 4 years of irritable mood, derobing behavior, physically and verbally aggressive actions, poor self-care, decreased food intake, disturbed sleep, and big talks. Investigation findings included affective resonance attenuation profile. He reports significant socio-occupational dysfunction and has a history of cannabis and nicotine use, characterized by craving, tolerance, and loss of control. Current treatment includes Sodium Valproate, Olanzapine, Cizodol-p5, and Chlorpromazine, which aim to manage his complex symptoms and enhance his overall functioning in daily life, effectively addressing both his mental health and social challenges while promoting stability and improvement.

A 27-year-old male presents with symptoms lasting 7 days, including increased goal-directed activity, grandiosity, questionable plans, increased demands, increased spending, easy distractibility, and decreased attention. Investigation findings included cognitive inflexibility signature. He has a history of similar episodes occurring twice previously. Current treatment includes two sessions of ECT and medications: Sodium Valproate, Risperidone, Lorazepam, and Chlorpromazine. No additional associated symptoms, stressors, or substance use are reported. The impact on functioning is not specified. The patient's mental status examination details are not provided. Further assessment may be necessary to evaluate the severity and implications of his current condition.

A 32-year-old male presents with increased talkativeness, elevated self-esteem, smiling to himself, and suspiciousness towards family members, lasting for 4 days. He has a history of cannabis use for 2 years, marked by craving, tolerance, and neglect of alternative activities. Investigation findings included attenuation pattern index. Current treatment includes Risperidone and Lorazepam. There are no reported stressors or associated symptoms. The impact on functioning is not specified. The onset of symptoms is unclear, and there is no past psychiatric history documented. The patient’s mental status examination and risk factors are not provided. Further assessment may be necessary to evaluate his condition.

A 28-year-old male presents with a one-month history of increased talkativeness, irritability, and aggressive behavior. He exhibits ideas of grandiosity, over-familiarity, cheerfulness, easy distractibility, heightened confidence, and elevated self-esteem. Investigation findings included reward prediction error sensitivity profile. These symptoms have persisted for two years, with a past history of similar episodes. Current treatment includes Amisulpride, Oxcarbazepine, and Lamotrigine. The patient’s functioning appears impacted, though specific details are not provided. No associated symptoms or stressors are noted, and there is no mention of substance use or risk factors. The clinical picture suggests a possible mood disorder with manic features.

A 51-year-old male presents with significant symptoms following a decrease in alcohol intake. He exhibits whole-body shaking, restlessness, irritability, and anger outbursts, alongside decreased sleep. His alcohol use history is marked by cravings, tolerance, and withdrawal, with continued use despite harm. Current treatment includes Lorazepam, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole, following discharge. The management plan incorporated Longitudinal Medication Monitoring Plan. The patient’s symptoms suggest a potential alcohol withdrawal syndrome, necessitating careful monitoring and support. The interplay between his substance use and psychological symptoms highlights the need for a comprehensive approach to address both his mental health and substance use issues effectively.

A 21-year-old male presents with a duration of 4 years and an onset of 2 weeks, experiencing significant fearfulness, apprehension, chest discomfort, low mood, decreased interest, disturbed sleep, agitation, and recurrent thoughts of self-harm and suicide, including self-harm behaviors such as strangulating himself and slapping himself. Risk assessment highlighted interpersonal conflict escalation scale. He has a past psychiatric history of three admissions for similar issues, indicating a chronic struggle with his mental health. Current treatment includes a tapered dose of Fluoxetine, the initiation of Venlafaxine with optimized doses, the addition of Risperidone, and a tapering of Quetiapine to address his symptoms effectively.

A 33-year-old female presents with a duration of 12 years and an onset of 3 weeks of persistent and pervasive low mood, worthlessness, hopelessness, feelings of guilt and burden, suicidal thoughts, low energy, decreased sleep, decreased appetite, and decreased activity. Risk assessment highlighted behavioral inhibition failure score. Her current treatment regimen includes Lithium 600mg, Sertraline 125mg, Quetiapine 150mg, Clobazam 5mg, Melatonin 5mg, and Propranolol 20mg, which are carefully selected to target her complex symptoms. The goal of this combination of medications is to enhance her overall mental health and alleviate the distressing symptoms she has been experiencing.

A 52-year-old female presents with significant socio-occupational dysfunction lasting one week. Investigation findings included attenuation pattern analysis. Core symptoms include fearfulness, delusion of persecution, shouting, muttering to herself, and decreased responsiveness. There are no reported associated symptoms or stressors. Current treatment consists of oral medications, specifically Olanzapine and Lorazepam. The patient’s condition has notably impacted her ability to function in social and occupational settings. Further assessment is necessary to evaluate the severity of her symptoms and the effectiveness of the current treatment regimen. Monitoring for potential side effects of medications is also recommended as part of her ongoing care.

A 50-year-old female presents with trembling of the whole body, responding to calling, saying she is a goddess, and chanting spells. She has experienced these core symptoms for 2 months, alongside anxiety symptoms, in the context of familial issues. Currently, she is being treated with Sertraline to address her symptoms and improve her overall mental health, aiming for better stability and functionality in her daily life as she navigates these challenges and seeks to regain control over her well-being, ultimately striving for a more balanced and fulfilling life amidst her ongoing struggles. Investigation findings included Neuroinflammatory Cytokine Index.

A 20-year-old male presents with an insidious onset of symptoms over the past three months. He reports decreased sleep and experiences delusions of persecution and reference. The patient also describes third-person auditory hallucinations and thought insertion. There are no noted associated symptoms, stressors, or substance use. The impact on functioning is not specified. Current treatment includes Clozapine, Clobazam, Propranolol, and Pantoprazole. Medication history included Psychapril. Further assessment is needed to evaluate the severity of symptoms and the effectiveness of the current medication regimen. Monitoring for side effects and adherence to treatment will be essential in ongoing management.

A 28-year-old male presents with a duration of 14 years of core symptoms, which include anger outbursts, violent behavior, increased anger, muttering to self, and hearing voices. He has a past history of multiple similar episodes that were treated with medications, but his current treatment regimen consists of Lithium 1200mg, Sodium Valproate 1600mg, Aripiprazole 40mg, Cizodol p5(5/2) 3 tabs, and Chlorpromazine 25mg. Medication history included servalpramine. Despite this extensive medication plan, the patient continues to experience significant challenges related to his symptoms, indicating a need for ongoing evaluation and potential adjustments in his treatment approach.

A 25-year-old female presents with a duration of 1 year of intentional self-harm by pouring petrol and burning herself, along with anger outbursts, mood swings, low mood, feelings of emptiness, unstable relationships, and impulsivity. She reports ongoing conflict in her relationship with her boyfriend and has a history of alcohol use, which complicates her situation further. Care planning emphasized clinical contact frequency gradient. Current treatment includes Olanzapine and Fluoxetine, as well as Clonazepam, aimed at addressing her symptoms, stabilizing her emotional state, and enhancing her coping mechanisms for improved overall mental health and well-being, ultimately seeking a path toward recovery and stability.

A 36-year-old male presents with core symptoms of muttering to self, picking behavior, decreased sleep, not recognizing people, vomiting, and agitation. He has a history of alcohol use characterized by craving, tolerance, and use despite harm, with recent stressors including abstinence from alcohol. Current treatment includes Lorazepam, Acamprosate, and Pantoprazole, and the duration and onset of his symptoms are critical for further evaluation to determine the most appropriate course of action, ensuring that all aspects of his condition are thoroughly assessed for effective management and optimal patient care moving forward. The management plan incorporated behavioral relapse surveillance plan.

A 41-year-old male presents with a 24-year history of alcohol use disorder. Core symptoms include craving, tolerance, loss of control, and withdrawal features. Clinical assessment identified frustration tolerance variability metric. Associated symptoms involve anger, aggressiveness, and auditory hallucinations, characterized by hearing voices and engaging in irrelevant talk. Current treatment includes Diazepam, recently changed to oral Lorazepam, along with Thiamine supplementation and Pantoprazole. The patient’s functioning impact and specific stressors are not documented. There is no mention of past history or risk factors. Mental status examination details are also absent, limiting further assessment of his current mental state.

A 62-year-old male presents with core symptoms of fearfulness, central chest pain, apprehension, sweating, and palpitations. Clinical assessment identified psychotic breakthrough probability index. There is no information regarding the duration, onset, or specific stressors contributing to his condition. The patient is currently being treated with Escitalopram, Clobazam, and Lurasidone. No details are provided about associated symptoms, functioning impact, substance use, risk factors, or past history. A mental status examination (MSE) has not been documented. The clinical picture suggests anxiety-related symptoms, warranting further assessment to understand the underlying causes and to evaluate the effectiveness of the current treatment regimen.

A 24-year-old female presents with a one-month history of low mood, decreased energy, and anhedonia. She reports restlessness, fearfulness, palpitations, shortness of breath, and continuous ruminations. Associated symptoms include frequent suicidal thoughts, feelings of hopelessness, helplessness, and worthlessness, along with sleep disturbances. Functional assessment highlighted cognitive reserve trajectory. Significant socio-occupational dysfunction is noted, primarily due to interpersonal relationship issues with her partner. Current medications include Oxcarbazepine 900mg, Olanzapine 10mg, Fluoxetine 40mg, Propranolol, and Melatonin. The patient expresses intent to die, indicating a high risk for self-harm. Ongoing assessment and intervention are necessary to address her mental health needs.

A 48-year-old female presents with symptoms lasting for 5 months, with an onset of 7 days. She exhibits muttering to herself, displays suspicion that her family is practicing black magic against her, and shows aggressive behavior towards them. The patient experiences delusions of reference, disturbed sleep, and auditory hallucinations involving derogatory comments. Current treatment includes Olanzapine and Clonazepam. There are no reported associated symptoms, stressors, or substance use. The impact on her functioning and any risk factors are not documented. A mental status examination and further evaluation are necessary for comprehensive assessment. Care planning emphasized service utilization efficiency metric.

A 40-year-old male presents with a 15-year history of alcohol use disorder characterized by a binge pattern of consumption, cravings, tolerance, loss of control, and withdrawal symptoms. Investigation findings included emotional regulation deficit pattern. He exhibits significant socio-occupational dysfunction, impacting his daily functioning. Current treatment includes IV Diazepam, Lorazepam, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole. There is no documented past history or specific stressors contributing to his condition. Mental status examination details are not provided. The patient’s ongoing struggle with alcohol use has led to considerable impairment in his social and occupational roles. Further assessment and intervention may be necessary to address his needs.

A 35-year-old male presents with a 15-year history of tolerance, loss of control, and withdrawal related to regular alcohol use. He exhibits abnormal body movement and has experienced a generalized tonic-clonic seizure following a decrease in intake 2 days prior to presentation. Current treatment includes Lorazepam, Acamprosate, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole to address his symptoms and support recovery, ensuring a comprehensive approach to his condition while promoting a safer path toward rehabilitation and long-term wellness, ultimately aiming to improve his quality of life and reduce the risk of future complications associated with alcohol dependence. The management plan incorporated clinical stability maintenance ratio.

A 28-year-old female presents with the ingestion of multiple prescribed drugs. She reports a recent dispute with her husband as a significant stressor contributing to her current situation. Notably, there is no past history of mood or anxiety symptoms, suggesting that this incident may be an isolated event rather than part of a chronic pattern, which could significantly influence her treatment and management moving forward, particularly in addressing the acute stressor and ensuring her safety, as well as evaluating her mental health needs in the context of this recent distress. The management plan incorporated remission maintenance consistency index.

A 66-year-old female presents with an insidious onset of symptoms over the past 9 months. She exhibits vacant staring, muttering to herself, and experiences delusions of reference and persecution. Care planning emphasized service linkage stability gradient. Additionally, she reports second-person auditory hallucinations of a commanding nature. Associated symptoms include over-religiosity and increased activity. Her condition has led to significant socio-occupational dysfunction. Past medical history includes hypothyroidism. Current treatment consists of Olanzapine 10 mg and Thyronorm. There are no reported stressors or substance use. A comprehensive mental status examination is warranted to further assess her condition and treatment efficacy.

A 31-year-old female presents with a duration of 6 months and an insidious onset of low mood, irritability, crying spells, and preoccupation regarding stressors, including conflict with her husband and familial stress. She has a significant history of intentional self-harm by hanging while under the influence of alcohol, along with a previous history of self-harm by cutting her wrist. Risk assessment highlighted self-injury frequency variability index. Currently, her treatment regimen includes Fluoxetine and Clonazepam, which aim to address her symptoms and enhance her overall mental health while providing necessary support during this challenging period in her life.

A 30-year-old male presents with irritability, aggressive behavior, restlessness, hallucinatory behavior, fearfulness, and disturbed sleep, persisting for 2 days. These symptoms coincide with a recent decrease in alcohol usage. The patient has a 10-year history of craving, tolerance, and withdrawal symptoms related to alcohol. Current medications include Lorazepam, Trazodone, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole. There is no additional information regarding functioning impact or risk factors. Investigation findings included fronto-amygdala dysconnectivity profile. The clinical picture suggests potential alcohol withdrawal or exacerbation of underlying psychiatric issues related to substance use. Further assessment and monitoring are recommended to evaluate symptom progression and treatment efficacy.

A 25-year-old female presents with a 3-month history of self-inflicted injury over the left wrist, depressed mood, feelings of worthlessness and hopelessness, disturbed sleep, decreased appetite, and decreased energy. Functional assessment highlighted interpersonal relationship satisfaction profile. The onset of symptoms was insidious, following the death of her partner, leading to significant socio-occupational dysfunction. She experiences recurrent thoughts about death. Past psychiatric history includes dissociative spells over the last 4 years. Current treatment includes Escitalopram, Clonazepam, and Melatonin. There are no reported substance use issues or additional associated symptoms. The clinical picture suggests a major depressive episode with possible complicated grief.

A 37-year-old female presents with a four-year history of psychiatric symptoms. She exhibits suspiciousness towards her family, aggressive behavior, and delusions of reference. Current treatment includes Risperidone and Haloperidol, supplemented with Benzhexol. There is no information regarding stressors, substance use, or past psychiatric history. The impact on her functioning is not specified. A mental status examination has not been provided. The patient's symptoms suggest a significant disturbance in perception and interpersonal relationships, warranting ongoing assessment and management of her medication regimen. Further evaluation may be necessary to understand her overall functioning and support needs. Care planning emphasized patient flow optimization ratio.

A 59-year-old male presents with an 11-day history of delusions of persecution towards co-workers and muttering to himself. He has a 27-year duration of symptoms, with current non-compliance noted as a stressor. Clinical assessment identified cognitive‑affective latency ratio. The patient is currently being treated with Aripiprazole and Lorazepam. There are no additional associated symptoms, substance use, or risk factors reported. The impact on functioning is not specified. A mental status examination (MSE) has not been provided, and there is no documented past psychiatric history. Further assessment is needed to evaluate the severity and implications of his symptoms.

A 45-year-old male presents with an irritable mood, poor social communication, and poor self-care. Functional assessment highlighted executive resilience index. He exhibits delusions of infidelity and persecution, alongside physical aggression towards family members. His sleep is disturbed, and he has decreased food intake. These symptoms contribute to significant socio-occupational dysfunction. Current treatment includes Olanzapine and Oxcarbazepine. There is no reported substance use or past psychiatric history. The patient’s condition necessitates ongoing evaluation and management to address the severity of his symptoms and their impact on his daily functioning. Further assessment may be required to monitor treatment efficacy and safety.

A 31-year-old male presents with a 15-year duration of irritable mood, delusion of persecution, increased religiosity, delusion of grandiosity, increased self-esteem, disturbed sleep, and refusal to take food. The onset was insidious, and his past history includes a depressive episode characterized by low mood, decreased interaction, decreased energy and activity, disturbed sleep, and decreased food intake for about one month approximately 15 years ago. His current treatment regimen includes Risperidone 8mg, Sodium Valproate 1500mg, and Chlorpromazine 100mg, which are aimed at effectively managing his ongoing symptoms and improving his overall mental health. Medication history included Lumazone.

A 37-year-old male presents with a 15-year history of alcohol consumption and 3 years of brown sugar use. He reports 2 days of restlessness, craving, tolerance, and withdrawal symptoms. Associated symptoms include decreased sleep, decreased appetite, and body ache. His substance use has led to neglect of other activities. Investigation findings included fronto-amygdala connectivity deficit. Current treatment includes Injection Tramadol, Pregabalin, Codeine, and Thiamine. The patient exhibits significant distress related to his substance use, impacting his daily functioning. Further assessment of his mental state and support systems is warranted to address his ongoing challenges with substance dependence.

The patient is a 42-year-old male who presented for evaluation after experiencing symptoms for 9 years. Clinical assessment identified psychomotor retardation velocity metric. He reported core symptoms including craving, tolerance, withdrawal, loss of control, tremulousness, sweating, disturbed sleep, agitation, disorientation, and irrelevant talking. His substance use history includes alcohol and nicotine. Current treatment consists of IV diazepam, oral lorazepam, acamprosate, and thiamine. Symptoms were described as ongoing and significantly impacting his daily functioning. The patient’s clinical presentation suggests a severe substance use disorder, necessitating comprehensive management and support to address his complex needs and improve his overall well-being.

A 35-year-old male presents with a 20-year history of alcohol use disorder. Core symptoms include cravings, withdrawal symptoms upon cessation, loss of control over drinking, and tolerance to alcohol. Clinical assessment identified cognitive load resilience threshold. Associated symptoms are also noted, including increased tolerance, cravings, and withdrawal symptoms. The patient reports concurrent use of cannabis and nicotine. Current treatment includes Lorazepam, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole. There is no information on stressors, functioning impact, past history, or risk factors. A mental status examination and further assessment may be necessary to evaluate the full extent of the patient's condition and treatment needs.

A 21-year-old female presents with an insidious onset over 3 years, characterized by an irritable mood, increased energy and activity, decreased need for sleep, increased talkativeness, distractibility, and impulsivity. Investigation findings included affective regulation signature. The mental status examination reveals increased PMA, along with an elevated rate, tone, and volume in speech, an irritable affect, and heightened self-esteem in thought. She has a past history of 3 episodes of mania and 1 episode of hypomania, and is currently treated with Sodium Valproate 1300mg, Lithium 1200mg, Quetiapine 300mg, and Haloperidol + Benzhexol (10/4mg) for her ongoing condition.

A 44-year-old female presents with a history of psychiatric symptoms lasting 18 years. Core symptoms include aggressive behavior, vacant staring, poor self-care, increased religiosity, irritability, disturbed sleep, delusions of persecution, decreased social interaction, muttering to self, and increased talkativeness. She has experienced multiple episodes in the past. Current treatment includes Risperidone, Sodium Valproate, and Trihexyphenidyl. Medication history included dexclamine. There is no information on stressors, substance use, or risk factors. The impact on functioning is not specified, and no details are provided regarding the mental status examination. Further assessment may be necessary to evaluate her condition comprehensively.

A 39-year-old male presents with a 9-year history of alcohol use disorder. Core symptoms include craving, tolerance, and withdrawal features. Associated symptoms are tremulousness, sweating, disturbed sleep, and fluctuating sensorium. Investigation findings included corticolimbic attenuation pattern. The condition has led to significant socio-occupational dysfunction. Current treatment includes Naltrexone, Lorazepam, Thiamine, Haloperidol, and Pantoprazole. There is no information on stressors, risk factors, mental status examination, or past history. The patient’s functioning is notably impaired due to the substance use, impacting both personal and professional aspects of life. Further assessment may be necessary to evaluate ongoing needs and treatment efficacy.

A 23-year-old female presents with a 5-month history of occasional low mood, crying spells, sleep disturbance, decreased energy, overthinking, helplessness, hopelessness, and suicidal thoughts. She reports interpersonal relationship issues with her family as a significant stressor. There is a past history of a similar episode 5 years ago, for which she took medications, though no documentation is available. Currently, she is being treated with Escitalopram 10mg. The impact on her functioning is not specified. Investigation findings included Limbic Stress Marker Panel. No information is provided regarding substance use or risk factors. Further assessment may be necessary to evaluate her condition comprehensively.

A 17-year-old female presents with a duration of 7 months and an onset of 5 months of headache, low mood, decreased energy, anhedonia, and anxiety symptoms. She has a troubling history of physical abuse from her family and has ingested multiple tablets of her prescribed medication, raising significant concerns about her safety and mental health. Medication history included valcalmine. Her current treatment regimen includes Escitalopram, Venlafaxine, Clonazepam, and Naproxen, which are aimed at effectively addressing her mental health concerns and improving her overall well-being while ensuring she receives the necessary support during this challenging time.

A 22-year-old female presents with elevated mood, decreased need for sleep, increased talkativeness, heightened self-esteem, increased energy level, and overfamiliarity. She has a history of bipolar type I disorder for 3 years, with multiple episodes previously, and has been experiencing these symptoms for 1 month. Current treatment includes Olanzapine 20mg, Haloperidol + Benzhexol (5/2), Lithium 1200mg, and Lorazepam in tapering dose, with a noted stressor of non-compliance to medication, which may be contributing to her current manic episode and exacerbating her condition, highlighting the importance of adherence to her prescribed regimen. Medication history included serapoxetine.

A 22-year-old female presents with symptoms that have persisted for 10 years, with an insidious onset. Her core symptoms include delusions of persecution, auditory hallucinations, restlessness, fearfulness, and a notable decrease in both sleep and appetite. Additionally, she experiences associated symptoms such as tolerance, craving, withdrawal, and continued substance use despite harm, specifically involving cannabis, opioids, and nicotine. Currently, she is receiving treatment that includes Tab Lorazepam, Tab Olanzapine, Tab Codeine, Tab Pregabalin, along with motivational enhancement therapy to address her complex needs. Medication history included cortireline.

A 29-year-old male presents with a 14-year history of symptoms, which include failure to conform to social norms, stealing, forging prescriptions, deceitfulness, repeated lying, and episodes of aggressiveness and irritability. Additionally, he demonstrates consistent irresponsibility, emotional detachment from family members, and a significant lack of remorse. The onset of these symptoms has been insidious, gradually developing over time, indicating a long-standing pattern of behavior. Currently, he is receiving treatment with Tab Lurasidone to address these ongoing issues and improve his overall functioning. Investigation findings included Inflammatory Response Index.

A 22-year-old male presents with symptoms that have persisted for 4 years, with an acute onset. His core symptoms include smiling to himself, muttering to himself, and experiencing anger over trivial issues, alongside diminished sleep, decreased interaction, and reduced self-care. Investigation findings included frontal-striatal connectivity deficit. Additionally, he reports cannabis use and is currently being treated with Tab Amisulpride and Tab Lorazepam. Overall, the clinical picture reflects significant challenges in his daily functioning, highlighting the impact of these symptoms on his quality of life.

A 48-year-old male presents with symptoms that have persisted for 2 months, following an acute onset. His core symptoms include sleep disturbances, delusions of persecution and reference, increased talk, mood lability, and aggressive behavior, all of which significantly impact his daily life. Investigation findings included Serum HPA Axis Ratio. Furthermore, his past medical history reveals grade II obesity and diabetes, conditions that may complicate his current mental health issues. To manage these troubling symptoms effectively, he is currently being treated with Tab Aripiprazole, which aims to alleviate his distress and improve his overall functioning.

A 40-year-old male presents with symptoms that have persisted for 9 years, with an insidious onset. His core symptoms include muttering to himself, social withdrawal, vacant staring, wandering away, decreased self-care, and decreased sleep. Assessment tools included emotion regulation latency index. Furthermore, he exhibits refusal to take food, unprovoked anger outbursts, and disorganized behavior, all of which contribute to his overall condition. Notably, he has a history of repeated episodes of similar illness in the past and is currently non-compliant with medication, with his current treatment regimen including Tab Olanzapine.
